Peregrine Review - Empires & Puzzles Hero Strategy Guide

Is Peregrine's unique 'Enhanced Dodge' enough to keep him relevant in 2026? We dive deep into the Woodland Faun's kit, exploring why his resistance to Insanity makes him a secret weapon against the maddening meta.
Peregrine: The Dodge-Dancing Faun Who Laughs at Insanity
Peregrine is a Legendary Nature hero from the

family, originally released in late 2024. As a Ranger, he brings a unique twist to the classic "dodge" mechanic, turning evasion into a finite resource rather than a timed buff. While he entered the game during the initial wave of "Wilderness" heroes, his specific toolkit—designed to counter the maddening "Insanity" mechanic—has kept him from fading into obscurity.

If you've ever wanted a hero who looks like he just skipped out of a midsummer night's dream to slap your enemies with a leaf-covered stick, Peregrine is your guy. Just don't let the smile fool you; he's surprisingly slippery.

Peregrine's design leans heavily into the "trickster spirit" archetype. He sports a pair of impressive ram horns, leafy attire that would make a druid jealous, and a dynamic, crouching pose that suggests he's ready to spring—or dodge—at a moment's notice. The color palette is a vibrant mix of emerald greens and earth tones, fitting perfectly with his Nature element. It's a lively, energetic design that captures the mischievous essence of a faun without feeling too cartoonish.

By early 2026 standards, Peregrine's base stats (Attack ~1135, Defense ~1110, Health ~2177) have noticeably aged. Modern heroes are now pushing 1300+ in attack and defense with health pools comfortably over 2400. While he isn't "glass cannon" fragile, he is no longer the stat-monster he was at launch. He relies heavily on his
to survive, rather than raw bulk. To use him competitively today, Limit Breaking (LB1 and preferably LB2) is almost mandatory to close the gap.
"Feyfire Slash" is a fascinating mix of offense and defensive utility.
450% damage to three targets is respectable. While it won't one-shot a modern team protected by massive overheal, it hits hard enough to soften up the flanks for a sniper to finish off.
This is his signature move. Unlike standard dodge which lasts for a set number of turns (e.g., 3 turns), Peregrine's dodge lasts until the ally successfully dodges
. This is incredibly powerful against teams that rely on a few big hits (like snipers). However, against teams with multi-hitters or rapid-fire heroes, those 5 charges can be burned through quickly.
If the dodge is removed (dispelled) or runs out, the ally gets a massive
. This creates a "damned if you do, damned if you don't" scenario for the defender. Dispel him, and you just enraged his team.
Average. With a level 23 Mana troop (or level 11 Magic/Cyclops), he charges in 9 tiles. He doesn't have inherent mana boosting passives, so he plays at standard speed.
Rough Damage Output: ~6,295 raw damage value (before defense mitigation) split across three targets.

effectively gives his team a coin-flip chance to ignore special skill damage. Statistically, avoiding 5 hits means avoiding potentially 5000+ incoming damage, which is far more valuable than a standard heal.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Woodland Faun family is one of the best "anti-meta" families in the game.

is gold. Even with just Peregrine (75% chance), he can often shrug off mana cuts from heroes like Li Xiu or the various water-themed mana stealers. Paired with another Faun, he becomes immune to mana manipulation, ensuring he fires exactly when you calculate he will.
A newer addition to his kit, this -60% accuracy for minions is a fantastic soft counter to minion-heavy teams, effectively neutralizing the chip damage that often decides close wars.
This is the big one. Insanity (Mindless Attack/Heal) is a debilitating mechanic. Peregrine is naturally immune. He will
attack his own team due to Insanity, making him a hard counter to the Cthulhu-mythos/Shadow heroes.
He ignores Attack Down ailments and Wither effects that target attack. This ensures his 450% hit is always hitting at full power.
fits his Ranger role perfectly. It helps mitigate his aging stats, giving him a flat damage boost at the start of the battle to ensure his first "Feyfire Slash" stings.
Upon release, Peregrine was considered a top-tier defensive flank. His dodge mechanic was frustratingly durable because it didn't expire with time.
As stats have ballooned, Peregrine's direct damage has become less threatening. He is no longer a feared "kill threat" on defense.

. You don't bring him for the damage; you bring him because the enemy team has Insanity heroes or heavy mana control. He is a specialized tool. In Rush Wars, his Average speed becomes Very Fast, and his Dodge becomes oppressive.

Don't pair him with other Dodge heroes (like C.Kadilen). The buffs will overwrite each other, and you lose the "Enhanced" mechanic of Peregrine's specific skill.
If you pulled Peregrine early, congratulations! He is an excellent carry hero. Focus on keeping him alive. Don't worry about his damage; treat him as a protector who occasionally slaps the enemy.
Peregrine is a niche counter-pick now. Don't use your Alpha Aethers on him unless you specifically lack counters for Insanity/Mana Control wars. He does his job fine at LB1.
When Peregrine was first released, the community was split. Some called the "charge-based dodge" broken, while others felt Average speed was too slow.
"The dodge is probably much better... I would prefer the mana ailment to the attack buff most of the time but perhaps the attack buff is better because there are still plenty of owls on defenses."
- A forum user debating the skill mechanics.
Many players noted that his art, while unique, has a distinct "mischievous" vibe that people either loved or found "weird."

Peregrine is the definition of a "technical" hero. He doesn't just bludgeon the enemy with raw stats; he outsmarts them. His immunity to the most frustrating mechanics in the game (Insanity and Mana Cut) means he will likely stay on your bench as a silver bullet for years to come, even if he isn't your daily driver.
A valuable specialist who has aged gracefully into a niche role. Keep him for the tricky wars, but maybe don't build your entire defense around him anymore.
A comprehensive review of Peregrine, the 5-star Nature hero from the Woodland Faun family. Analyze his Enhanced Dodge, powerful passives, and modern viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
